commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a `.github/deadcode.allow` baseline and a CI step that runs `golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.45.0`, normalizes output, and fails on unexpected or stale findings.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a CI-policy PR, not a runtime bug report. I checked current main and the branch diff instead of reproducing a user-facing failure.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human maintainer review is the remaining step because this member-authored draft adds an enforced CI analyzer and baseline, not because there is a narrow repair for automation.

target.title: ci: add deadcode check
target.body:
## Summary
- add an enforced Go deadcode CI gate pinned to `golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode@v0.45.0`
- preserve current downstream/public library API surface with `.github/deadcode.allow`
- normalize deadcode output before allowlist comparison so OS/toolchain line-number drift does not break CI
- fail CI on new deadcode findings or stale allowlist entries

## Validation
- `git diff --check`
- `GOWORK=off` deadcode gate command with normalized allowlist comparison
- `GOWORK=off go test -count=1 ./...`
- `autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --no-web-search --thinking low` clean
target.author: vincentkoc
